# Security Finding: Potential GitHub Personal Access Token

| Field       | Value                              |
|-------------|------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | IRON-007                                 |
| **Severity**| CRITICAL                                 |
| **Status**  | REJECTED — NOT EXPLOITABLE                                 |
| **Confidence** | 0%                            |
| **CWE**     | CWE-798                                 |
| **CVSS**    | 9.8                               |

## 📋 Executive Summary

**Verdict:** NOT EXPLOITABLE (confidence: 0%)

The scanner detected a critical severity hardcoded secret in `secrets.py:7`. This finding was identified by step 4 (Hardcoded secrets) of the Ironwall pipeline. AI-powered analysis (attack path construction, evidence verification, exploitability assessment) will be applied when the Agent Engine is fully integrated.

## 📖 Analysis Narrative

The scanner detected a critical severity hardcoded secret in `secrets.py:7`. This finding was identified by step 4 (Hardcoded secrets) of the Ironwall pipeline. AI-powered analysis (attack path construction, evidence verification, exploitability assessment) will be applied when the Agent Engine is fully integrated.

## 🔍 Evidence

### Evidence 1: scanner (likely confidence)

Regex pattern detected a potential GitHub Personal Access Token at secrets.py:7. This may be a hardcoded secret that automated scanners missed.

**Location:** `secrets.py:7`

```
  7 | GITHUB_TOKEN = "ghp_1A2b3C4d5E6f7G8h9I0jK1lM2nO3pQ4r5S6t"
```



## 🎯 Attack Path

An attacker could exploit this finding through the following steps:

**Step 1:** Attacker profile: Anyone with access to the source code (`secrets.py:7`)

**Step 2:** Attack path: Read the file, extract the credential, use it to access the target service

**Step 3:** Business impact: Unauthorized access to the protected service



## ✅ Verification

**Verification:** Not independently verified.

**Method:** scanner-only

**Detail:** Finding from traditional scanner pipeline. AI Agent verification not yet applied (coming Day 7).

## 🔧 Remediation

_No fix suggestion available._

---

*Report generated by Ironwall Agent Engine v0.5.0*

### Raw Scanner Findings

- **hardcoded-secret** (step-4) — `secrets.py:7`

# Security Finding: Kubernetes privileged container in docker-compose.yml

| Field       | Value                              |
|-------------|------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | IRON-016                                 |
| **Severity**| CRITICAL                                 |
| **Status**  | REJECTED — NOT EXPLOITABLE                                 |
| **Confidence** | 0%                            |
| **CWE**     | CWE-16                                 |
| **CVSS**    | 9.8                               |

## 📋 Executive Summary

**Verdict:** NOT EXPLOITABLE (confidence: 0%)

The scanner detected a critical severity insecure configuration in `docker-compose.yml:11`. This finding was identified by step 6 (Server & IaC configuration) of the Ironwall pipeline. AI-powered analysis (attack path construction, evidence verification, exploitability assessment) will be applied when the Agent Engine is fully integrated.

## 📖 Analysis Narrative

The scanner detected a critical severity insecure configuration in `docker-compose.yml:11`. This finding was identified by step 6 (Server & IaC configuration) of the Ironwall pipeline. AI-powered analysis (attack path construction, evidence verification, exploitability assessment) will be applied when the Agent Engine is fully integrated.

## 🔍 Evidence

### Evidence 1: scanner (likely confidence)

Container runs in privileged mode or allows privilege escalation. Container escape risk.
Found at line 11: privileged: true  # dangerous

**Location:** `docker-compose.yml:11`

```
  11 | privileged: true  # dangerous
```



## 🎯 Attack Path

_No attack path constructed. The finding could not be linked to an exploitable chain._

## ✅ Verification

**Verification:** Not independently verified.

**Method:** scanner-only

**Detail:** Finding from traditional scanner pipeline. AI Agent verification not yet applied (coming Day 7).

## 🔧 Remediation

### Recommended Fix

Set privileged: false and allowPrivilegeEscalation: false.

**CWE Reference:** [CWE-16](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/16.html)

---

*Report generated by Ironwall Agent Engine v0.5.0*

### Raw Scanner Findings

- **insecure-configuration** (step-6) — `docker-compose.yml:11`

---

# Security Finding: Exposed Docker daemon socket in docker-compose.yml

| Field       | Value                              |
|-------------|------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | IRON-017                                 |
| **Severity**| CRITICAL                                 |
| **Status**  | REJECTED — NOT EXPLOITABLE                                 |
| **Confidence** | 0%                            |
| **CWE**     | CWE-16                                 |
| **CVSS**    | 9.8                               |

## 📋 Executive Summary

**Verdict:** NOT EXPLOITABLE (confidence: 0%)

The scanner detected a critical severity insecure configuration in `docker-compose.yml:16`. This finding was identified by step 6 (Server & IaC configuration) of the Ironwall pipeline. AI-powered analysis (attack path construction, evidence verification, exploitability assessment) will be applied when the Agent Engine is fully integrated.

## 📖 Analysis Narrative

The scanner detected a critical severity insecure configuration in `docker-compose.yml:16`. This finding was identified by step 6 (Server & IaC configuration) of the Ironwall pipeline. AI-powered analysis (attack path construction, evidence verification, exploitability assessment) will be applied when the Agent Engine is fully integrated.

## 🔍 Evidence

### Evidence 1: scanner (likely confidence)

Docker socket is mounted in container. This grants container root access to the host.
Found at line 16: - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock  # Docker socket exposed

**Location:** `docker-compose.yml:16`

```
  16 | - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock  # Docker socket exposed
```



## 🎯 Attack Path

_No attack path constructed. The finding could not be linked to an exploitable chain._

## ✅ Verification

**Verification:** Not independently verified.

**Method:** scanner-only

**Detail:** Finding from traditional scanner pipeline. AI Agent verification not yet applied (coming Day 7).

## 🔧 Remediation

### Recommended Fix

Remove Docker socket mount unless absolutely necessary. Use docker-api-proxy instead.

**CWE Reference:** [CWE-16](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/16.html)

---

*Report generated by Ironwall Agent Engine v0.5.0*

### Raw Scanner Findings

- **insecure-configuration** (step-6) — `docker-compose.yml:16`

---

# Security Finding: Docker running as root in Dockerfile

| Field       | Value                              |
|-------------|------------------------------------|
| **ID**      | IRON-015                                 |
| **Severity**| HIGH                                 |
| **Status**  | CONFIRMED — EXPLOITABLE                                 |
| **Confidence** | 0%                            |
| **CWE**     | CWE-16                                 |
| **CVSS**    | 7.5                               |

## 📋 Summary

**Verdict:** EXPLOITABLE (confidence: 0%)

The scanner detected a high severity insecure configuration in `Dockerfile:7`. This finding was identified by step 6 (Server & IaC configuration) of the Ironwall pipeline. AI-powered analysis (attack path construction, evidence verification, exploitability assessment) will be applied when the Agent Engine is fully integrated.

## 🔍 Evidence

### Evidence 1: scanner (likely confidence)

Docker container runs as root. If compromised, attacker gets full container privileges.
Found at line 7: USER root

**Location:** `Dockerfile:7`

```
  7 | USER root
```



## 🎯 Attack Path

_No attack path constructed. The finding could not be linked to an exploitable chain._

## 🔧 Remediation

### Recommended Fix

Add USER directive with a non-root user.

**CWE Reference:** [CWE-16](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/16.html)

---

*Report generated by Ironwall Agent Engine v0.5.0*

### Raw Scanner Findings

- **insecure-configuration** (step-6) — `Dockerfile:7`
